prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the third quarter of the year of our lord two thousand and twenty-two, the company known as SaaS didst reporteth a revenue of twenty million dollars, a sum most bountiful and exceeding the expectations of the wise men and women who doth analyze such matters. The number of users who didst partake in the company's services didst grow by a mighty thirty percent, a feat most impressive and worthy of note in the annals of history. The company's profits didst increase by a whopping fifty percent, a sign of the favor of the gods upon this enterprise. And lo, the company's stock price didst rise by a staggering twenty percent, a testament to the wisdom of those who didst invest their hard-earned coin in this most promising of ventures.
